title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: A powerful coming-of-age novel set in the American South, exploring justice, prejudice, courage, and moral growth through the eyes of young Scout Finch.

author: George Orwell
description: A dystopian novel about surveillance, propaganda, thought control, and one man's struggle against a totalitarian regime.

title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: A Jazz Age classic about wealth, obsession, illusion, and the fragile dream surrounding Jay Gatsby and Daisy Buchanan.

title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: A witty romantic classic following Elizabeth Bennet as she navigates love, family pressure, class expectations, and first impressions.

title: The Catcher in the Rye
author: J. D. Salinger
description: A landmark coming-of-age novel following Holden Caulfield as he wanders New York City and wrestles with alienation, grief, and adulthood.

title: The Hobbit
author: J. R. R. Tolkien
description: Bilbo Baggins leaves his comfortable home for a grand adventure with dwarves, trolls, goblins, riddles, treasure, and a dragon named Smaug.

title: Fahrenheit 451
author: Ray Bradbury
description: A dystopian story about a future society where books are banned and burned, following fireman Guy Montag as he begins to question everything.

title: Moby-Dick
author: Herman Melville
description: An epic sea voyage led by Captain Ahab, whose obsessive hunt for the white whale becomes a meditation on fate, revenge, and human ambition.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A philosophical tale about Santiago, a shepherd boy who follows a dream across the desert in search of treasure and personal destiny.

title: The Book Thief
author: Markus Zusak
description: Set in Nazi Germany, this moving novel follows Liesel Meminger, a young girl who finds comfort and resistance through stolen books and words.

title: The Kite Runner
author: Khaled Hosseini
description: A deeply emotional story of friendship, betrayal, guilt, and redemption set against decades of Afghan history.

title: The Road
author: Cormac McCarthy
description: A bleak yet tender post-apocalyptic novel about a father and son traveling through a ruined world while trying to preserve hope and humanity.

title: The Hunger Games
author: Suzanne Collins
description: Katniss Everdeen is forced into a televised fight for survival in Panem, a brutal nation ruled by spectacle, fear, and rebellion.

title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
author: J. K. Rowling
description: Harry Potter discovers he is a wizard and begins his first year at Hogwarts, where friendship, magic, danger, and mystery await.
